records.ety: [Said to be from a song with the same verse construction, current in Ireland, the refrain of which contains the place name Limerick.]

defs: A humorous, often nonsensical, and sometimes risq poem of five anapestic lines, of which lines 1, 2, and 5 are of three feet, and rhyme, and lines 3 and 4 are of two feet, and rhyme.
quotes: There was a young lady, Amanda, Whose Ballades Lyriques were quite fin de Sicle, I deem But her Journal Intime Was what sent her papa to Uganda.
notes: It often begins with "There once was a . . ." or "There was a . . ."; as --
